posts.text: China's is bailing out the global oil market: Is Beijing coordinating with Washington? Or is it taking care of itself, with the global benefit a byproduct? My @Opinion column on China (May 7) is below.
posts.summary: Bloomberg's Javier Blas asks whether China is effectively propping up the global oil market by absorbing barrels, or simply acting in self-interest with the stabilizing effect a byproduct.

posts.text: According to the @NYTimes, US President Trump has toughened the terms of a potential framework for a deal to end the war in Iran, and has sent those proposed changes back to the country for consideration.
posts.summary: Per the NYT, President Trump has hardened the terms of a proposed framework to end the Iran war and sent the revised deal back to Tehran, a key swing factor for the Strait of Hormuz and the oil risk premium.

posts.text: RT @kparpiani: Qatar opposes permanent legal fees for transit through the Strait of Hormuz, but a temporary fee is negotiable and could help...
posts.summary: Javier Blas shares analysis that Qatar opposes permanent transit fees through the Strait of Hormuz but views a temporary fee as negotiable, hinting that a framework to reopen the chokepoint is possible.

posts.text: China factory activity stalls in May as demand weakens
posts.summary: Reuters reports China's factory activity stalled in May as demand weakened, a bearish signal for Chinese oil and commodity demand.

posts.text: Israel troops capture Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon push against Hezbollah
posts.summary: Reuters reports Israeli troops captured Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon in a push against Hezbollah, keeping Middle East escalation risk and its oil-market premium elevated.

posts.text: The future of AI development may depend as much on electricity infrastructure as computing power.
posts.summary: Wood Mackenzie highlights that US AI growth increasingly hinges on power infrastructure, with 90+ GW of collocated generation seeking grid connection as data centers drive 'bring your own generation' models.
